summary:
The issue was caused by overlapping segment assignments: the original `segments_for_worker` function gave each worker two extra segments, so multiple threads wrote to the same byte ranges. I updated the function to distribute segments uniquely (and handle any remainder segments). After rebuilding and running `test.sh`, both the integrity and overlap checks pass:

```
=== Concurrent Write Integrity Test ===
--- Building ---
   Compiling concurrent-writer v0.1.0 (/app)
    Finished `release` profile [optimized] target(s) in 0.18s
VERIFY: File integrity check passed
--- Checking segment assignment overlap ---
OK: No overlapping segment assignments
PASS: All concurrent write runs produced correct output
```

The “needle” has been found and fixed.
